title: Tunable positions of Weyl nodes via magnetism and pressure in the ferromagnetic Weyl semimetal CeAlSi

paper_analysis.summary: This paper studies the ferromagnetic Weyl semimetal CeAlSi using transport, thermoelectric, ARPES, high-pressure XRD, and DFT calculations. The main finding is that both ferromagnetism and pressure tune the positions of Weyl nodes without changing their classification, which correlates with changes in anomalous Hall/Nernst responses and multiple pressure-induced phase transitions.

figures.description: Schematic crystal structure of CeAlSi in the noncentrosymmetric I41md space group.
Calculated electronic band structure and associated Berry curvature.
Zero-field longitudinal resistivity and ZFC/FC magnetization versus temperature, showing the ferromagnetic transition near 10.1 K.
Hall resistivity versus magnetic field at different temperatures.
Extracted anomalous Hall resistivity versus field at various temperatures after subtracting the linear ordinary Hall background.
Magnetization versus field at various temperatures.
Calculated anomalous Hall conductivity and anomalous Hall angle versus temperature.
Normalized magneto-Seebeck signal versus field at different temperatures.
Nernst signal versus field at different temperatures.
Nernst signal normalized to temperature; inset shows anomalous Nernst amplitude and anomalous Nernst angle.
Contour plots comparing anomalous Hall conductivity, anomalous Nernst conductivity, and dM/dH.
Angle-integrated resonant and off-resonant photoemission spectra at the Ce N edge, highlighting Ce 4f resonance.
Core-level photoemission spectra showing Al-2p and Si-2p peaks.
Sketch of bulk and (001)-projected surface Brillouin zones.
Constant-energy ARPES map near EF at low temperature with marked momentum cuts.
Constant-energy ARPES map above TC.
Constant-energy ARPES map below TC.
Second-derivative ARPES band dispersion along cut #a above and below TC, showing α-band splitting.
Second-derivative ARPES band dispersion along cut #b above and below TC, showing β-band splitting.
Second-derivative ARPES band dispersion along cut #c above TC with calculated bands overlaid and Weyl point WP1 indicated.
Second-derivative ARPES band dispersion along cut #d above TC with calculated bands overlaid and Weyl point WP2 indicated.
Same as Fig2i below TC, showing increased Weyl-node separation.
Same as Fig2j below TC, showing increased Weyl-node separation.
Momentum distribution curves at EF along cut #c used to extract Fermi momentum splitting.
Momentum distribution curves at -50 meV along cut #d used to extract Fermi momentum splitting.
Temperature-dependent resistivity under various pressures, with normalized low-temperature resistivity shown on the right.
ac magnetic susceptibility under pressure.
Hall resistivity at various pressures.
Extracted anomalous Hall resistivity versus pressure.
Contour plot of derivative of normalized resistivity versus temperature and pressure, with transition temperatures overlaid.
Pressure-dependent Hall coefficient and 2 K resistivity.
Pressure dependence of anomalous Hall angle and absolute anomalous Hall conductivity.
High-pressure XRD patterns up to 60 GPa showing emergence of a new diffraction peak above ~39.3 GPa.
Rietveld refinement of the XRD pattern at 1.0 GPa.
Pressure dependence of normalized lattice parameters and a/c ratio extracted from XRD refinements.
Calculated band structure along Γ-W-X at 0 GPa.
Calculated band structure along Γ-W-X at 10 GPa.
Calculated band structure along Γ-W-X at 20 GPa.
Calculated 3D Fermi surface at 0 GPa.
Calculated 3D Fermi surface at 10 GPa.
Calculated 3D Fermi surface at 20 GPa.

paper_analysis.raw_measurement_details: Raw measurements include resistivity, Hall resistivity, magnetization, ac magnetic susceptibility, thermoelectric signals (Seebeck and Nernst), ARPES spectra and maps, and high-pressure XRD patterns. The paper also mentions compositional analysis and Laue diffraction for crystal characterization.
paper_analysis.processed_plot_details: Processed plots include anomalous Hall resistivity after subtracting ordinary Hall background, anomalous Hall conductivity, anomalous Hall angle, anomalous Nernst amplitude/angle, second-derivative ARPES band images, momentum distribution curves, normalized resistivity derivatives, Rietveld-refined lattice parameters, and DFT-derived band structures/Fermi surfaces.

dataset_characterization.data_availability_statement: The paper states that the data supporting the findings are available from the corresponding authors upon request.
dataset_characterization.raw_data_description: Raw data are described only at the level of measurement modalities and instruments in the Methods and figure captions; no file formats, repository links, or explicit raw-data archive are given.
dataset_characterization.processed_data_description: Processed data are described as background-subtracted anomalous Hall signals, normalized transport quantities, extracted transition temperatures, Rietveld-refined lattice constants, and DFT-calculated electronic structures and magnetic moments.
dataset_characterization.figure_data_description: Figure-specific source data are implied by the plotted curves and maps, but the paper text does not explicitly state that source data files are deposited. Supplementary information is referenced for additional details and likely contains supporting plots/data, but this is not explicit in the provided text.
dataset_characterization.scripts_description: No analysis scripts, notebooks, or code repositories are mentioned in the paper text provided.
dataset_characterization.replot_reason: The paper does not provide a public data repository, explicit source-data files, or downloadable raw datasets in the text; access is only by request to the authors, so direct replotting from the paper text alone is not possible.
dataset_characterization.notes: The dataset is multi-modal and figure-driven, combining experimental raw measurements with substantial post-processing and theory outputs. The text strongly indicates derived quantities and fitted/extracted parameters, but not a structured public dataset. Supplementary information is repeatedly referenced for methodological details and additional plots.

classification_prior.raw_data_evidence: The paper reports direct measurements from transport, thermoelectric, ARPES, ac susceptibility, and high-pressure XRD experiments, plus DFT calculations.
classification_prior.source_data_evidence: The text does not explicitly mention deposited source-data files or a source-data archive.
classification_prior.data_availability_section_relevant: “The data that support the ﬁndings of this study are available from the corresponding authors upon request.”; Methods mention details in Supplementary Information.
classification_prior.review_notes: Be careful not to classify derived anomalous Hall/Nernst quantities, second-derivative ARPES images, or Rietveld-refined parameters as raw data. The paper supports raw experimental acquisition, but the accessible dataset semantics in the text are limited and do not indicate a public source-data package.
